name: Darkness over Daggerford
author: Unknown
description:
Darkness over Daggerford — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to 1372 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Dukes of Baldur's Gate
author: Unknown
description:
Dukes of Baldur's Gate — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to 1492 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Dwarven Runes: A Quick Guide to Interpretation
author: Carorn Forath
description:
Dwarven Runes: A Quick Guide to Interpretation — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Carorn Forath is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1370 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Dethek: Dialects and Diction
author: Unknown
description:
Dethek: Dialects and Diction — An in-universe work listed in Candlekeep indices and frontier libraries throughout the Heartlands. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to unknown.

Copies vary by scriptorium; Candlekeep treats the title as known though completeness is never guaranteed.

name: Delblood's Atlas of Faerûn
author: Delblood
description:
Delblood's Atlas of Faerûn — A regional survey written for travellers who cannot afford to learn geography from dead companions. Delblood is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Maps are absent or obsolete in most copies; readers are expected to update the text with their own ink.

name: Demonomicon of Iggwilv
author: Iggwilv
description:
Demonomicon of Iggwilv — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Iggwilv is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to unknown.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.
